Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting significant renal impairment. Low total CO₂ may indicate metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), supporting renal dysfunction and possible tubular injury. Immediate nephrology evaluation is recommended for further assessment and management.
